title: gratiola fluviatilis
intro: Gratiola fluviatilis, also known as Marsh Felwort, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the southeastern United States, particularly in marshy and wetland areas. It has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Gratiola fluviatilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for anxiety and stress relief. Gratiola fluviatilis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
